title: Container append (move-in)
pattern: Appending an _Owned element to a container moves it into the container's Vec via push; after the call the caller's handle is gone — do not use or free it.
tags: array append add owned move vec push element item

title: Mutate through &_Mut
pattern: Take the container by a mutable borrow (non-const *_Borrow / &_Mut), mutate it through a method; the borrow ends at the call and ownership stays with the caller.
tags: mutate modify append add set container borrow mut

title: Read through &_Const
pattern: Read a container's size or contents through a const borrow (const *_Borrow); no mutation, no ownership transfer, safe to call repeatedly.
tags: read size count get contents const borrow immutable

title: Lookup returns _Nullable
pattern: A lookup that may miss returns a _Nullable borrow of the child; the caller must null-check before dereferencing. Never return an owning pointer from a lookup.
tags: lookup find get object key member nullable optional absent

title: Constructor returns _Owned
pattern: A constructor safe_mallocs a node, initializes its fields, and returns an _Owned _Nonnull pointer; the caller owns it and must eventually Delete it (or move it into a container).
tags: create constructor new make owned return allocate

title: Recursive destructor frees the tree
pattern: An _Owned struct's destructor frees each owned field and recurses into container children, so deleting the root frees the whole tree exactly once — no leak, no double free.
tags: delete free destroy destructor recursive tree owned cleanup

title: libcbs String length includes the NUL
pattern: String::from(cstr) stores strlen+1 chars: String::length() INCLUDES the trailing '\0'. When comparing a String against a C string (or iterating characters), trim the trailing NUL first (n>0 && at(n-1)=='\0' -> n-=1), or lookups silently miss.
tags: string length nul terminator compare cstr key lookup from strlen
